Such information may change from time to time and details of the changes will appear on that website.

General information on the Index The CCX HK -US Dividend Low Volatility Index (HKD Net Return) is a cross -market equity index designed to track dividend -yielding securities eligible for Southbound Stock Connect or listed on major US exchanges.
Guided by the principles of stable dividends a nd superior quality, the Index selects securities with stable income characteristics and low volatility from both markets.

Through a structured cross -market weighting framework and factor -based selection mechanism, the Index aims to provide investors with a dividend-oriented investment tool that balances enhanced returns and risk diversification, reflecting the overall performance of high -quality dividend companies in Hong Kong and the US.

The Index is a net total return index which means that the performance of the Index constituents is calculated on the basis that any dividends or distributions are reinvested after withholding tax deduction (if any).
The Index is denominated and quoted in HKD.
The Index was launched on 30 September 202 5.
The base date for the Index was 3 1 December 2016 with a base level of 1,000 as of the base date.

The Index is calculated and disseminated real -time during the trading hours of the SEHK and the last closing Index level and other important news relating to the Index can be found on the Index Provider’s website at https://www.ccxindices.com/indexDetails?code=CCX50596 (this website has not been reviewed by the SFC) .
As at 23 January 2026, the Index had a total market capitalisation of approximately HKD 60,382.72 billion and 60 constituents.
The largest constituent of the Index accounted for 5.12% of the Index.

Index methodology The Index constituents and their respective weightings are accessible and published after each Index rebalancing on a retrospective basis and in advance of the next rebalancing on the Index Provider’s website at https://www.ccxindices.com/indexDetails?code=CCX50596 (this website has not been reviewed by the SFC) .

Index universe The Index universe consists of Securities listed on the SEHK that are eligible for Southbound trading under the Stock Connect, as well as common stocks listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), the NASDAQ Stock Market (NASDAQ) and NYSE American.

Constituent selection Constituent selection is conducted separately for Hong Kong listed Securities and US listed Securities, using predefined and objective eligibility criteria and ranking rules as further described below.

211 Hong Kong listed Securities Hong Kong listed Securities meeting the following criteria are selected as eligible candidates: (a) the average daily trading value (“ ADTV”) over both the past 3 months and the past 1 -year ranks within the top 50% of the securities eligible for Southbound Stock Connect; (b) having a 1 -year average market capitalisation ranking within the top 30% of the securities eligible for Southbound Stock Connect; and (c) having made consecutive cash dividend payments in each of the past 3 fiscal years (but excluding Securities ranked in the highest 20% by volatility of dividend payout ratios over the past 3 years).

A quality factor score is calculated for each eligible Security, which is the average of the scores assigned for each of the 5 primary factors, namely low valuation factor, earning quality factor, beta factor, dividend factor and momentum factor.
These 5 p rimary factors are of the same weighting (20% each).
A primary factor may also have sub-factors (i.e.
secondary factors) as further provided in the Index methodology, in which case the score for such primary factor will be the sum of the scores assigned for its corresponding secondary factor(s).
Where there are secondary factors, the score for a primary factor is the average of the sub -scores assigned for each secondary factor.
Likewise, each sub-score has the same weighting.
The top 30 eligible Securities ranked in descending order by quality factor score are selected for inclusion in the Index (“ HK Index Securities ”).
US listed Securities U.S.

listed Securities meeting the following criteria are selected as eligible candidates: (a) listed for at least 1 year, ranking within the top 30% of the universe by average daily market capitalisation and within the top 50% by ADTV over the past 1 year; (b) having made consecutive cash dividend payments in each of the past 5 fiscal years and a non-negative dividend growth rate over the past 3 years; (c) having a dividend yield of no more than 10% based on the most recent fiscal year; and (d) having a positive shareholders’ equity as of the latest reporting period.

A quality factor score is calculated for each eligible Security, which is the average of the scores assigned for each of the two primary factors, namely return on equity (ROE) factor and low volatility factor.
These 2 primary factors are of the same weight ing (50% each).
The top 30 eligible Securities ranked in descending order by quality factor score are selected for inclusion in the Index (“ US Index Securities ”).
Constituent weighting Initial Index weights of HK Index Securities are determined using a rank -based factor weighting approach.

The weight of a HK Index Security ( Wi,t) is determined based on the following formula: 𝑊𝑖,𝑡 = 𝑟𝑎𝑛𝑘𝑖,𝑡 ∑ 𝑟𝑎𝑛𝑘𝑖,𝑡 30 𝑖=1 where 𝑟𝑎𝑛𝑘𝑖,𝑡 for the HK Index Security equals to the ascending rank of its quality factor score divided by the number of Index Securities (30), such that the HK Index Securities with higher quality factor scores receive higher weights.
In the unlikely event that two or more HK Index Securities have the exact same quality factor score, the HK Index Security with a larger market capitalisation shall be 212 assigned a higher ranking.
Meanwhile, the maximum weight of a single industry and a single HK Index Security are limited to 35% and 7.7% of the sub-portfolio of HK Index Securities respectively.
For US Index Securities, initial Index weights are determined based on market capitalisation weighting.
The weight of any single US Index Security is capped at 14.29% of the sub -portfolio of US Index Securities.

The Index constituents are finalised by combining the selected HK Index Securities and US Index Securities (based on the above weighting), then further adjusted such that the aggregate weight for HK Index Securities equals to 65% of the Index portfolio, wh ile the aggregate weight for US Index Securities equals to 35% of the Index portfolio.
A cap of 5% is imposed on the weighting of any individual Index Security in the Index at the time of Index rebalancing.

Index calculation The Index is calculated and published in real -time every 15 seconds during Hong Kong and US stock trading sessions on each day on which the exchanges in Hong Kong and US are open for normal trading, using an adjusted market capitalisation methodology: Index Level = ∑(Price × Adjusted Shares × Weight Factor) Divisor × 1000 The calculation of adjusted share capital and the divisor adjustment are determined in accordance with the Calculation and Maintenance Rules.
The value of Weight Factor is between 0 and 1.
Index maintenance and adjustments The Index constituents are reviewed quarterly on the second day on which both the Stock Connect and U.S.
stock markets are open in March, June, September and December each year, and the rebalancing becomes effective on the first common trading day of the Stock Connect and U.S.
stock markets following the second Friday of the month.
For the purpose of facilitating ad -hoc Index adjustments between regular rebalancing dates, a reserve list is established during each regular review.
The number of Securities in the reserve list equals to 40% of the number of Index Securities.
Ad hoc adjustments to the Index may be implemented under special circumstances.

An Index Security shall be removed from the Index on the next trading day if it is delisted, suspended from trading for an extended period, subject to regulatory risk warnings, or becomes ineligible for inclusion in the Hong Kong Stock Connect.
In such cases, a replacement Security shall be selected from the reserve list in accordance with the Index methodology.

Treatment of corporate actions, including acquisitions, mergers, and spin -offs involving Index Securities, shall be conducted in accordance with the Calculation and Maintenance Rules of the Index Provider at https://www.ccxindices.com/compliance/details?id=811 (this website has not been reviewed by the SFC).

If the aggregate weight of US Index Securities exceeds 35% as of 3 trading days prior to the effective date (T -3 Day), the Index shall undergo an ad -hoc adjustment during the regular review months of March and September in order to restore compliance with the Index constraints.
